title: Connecting     HTC Vive in SV location
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8j8qh","text":"Connecting the HTC Vive for VR play while mirrored on television.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"482ec","text":"Double check that the Titan workstation is not reserved by anyone else.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Save all work on the laptop.
While the laptop is restarting, get the corded HTC Vive from the display.
Right-click on the speaker icon and choose "Sound settings."
Attach HTC Vive to Titan laptop.
Take the headset out of the Cleanbox and place it on the table next to the Titan laptop.
Using the Vive 3-in-1 cable (tips ringed with orange), connect to the HTC Vive Link Box attached to the laptop.
Grab the controllers off the charger and place next to the headset, ensuring they're in line-of-sight of the base stations.
Start Steam on the Titan laptop.
Close any pop-ups that may have opened (Steam will typically show an ad after starting).
Ensure the mavrxlab Steam account is signed in.
Make sure the SteamVR icons are lit up: headset, two controllers, and two sensors. SteamVR icons
Select Library in Steam, choose the program you wish to run, and hit the Play button.
Use the Cleanbox protocol on the headset and controllers. Cleanbox UV-C sterilization chamber by Ryan Straight
creator: Ryan Straight
